figures.caption: Fig. 2: Side view of the PPW-fed metasurface antenna comprising one feed and a single elliptic iris at the center of the structure. The feed is a thin wire, which is assumed to be excited by a digital source along with an RFC.

Fig. 8: FF directivity patterns versus the elevation angle θ for different azimuth planes: (a) φ = 0◦, (b) φ = 90◦, (c) φ = 180◦, and (d) φ = 270◦. The directivity retrieved from FW simulations is compared with the proposed electric and magnetic dipoles model (Section III) as well as the magnetic-dipoles-only approach (Section II).

Fig. 9: FF directivity pattern versus the azimuth angle φ, considering the elevation angle θ = 75◦. The electric and magnetic dipoles model (Section III) and the magnetic-dipolesonly one (Section II) are compared with FW simulations.

Fig. 6: 3D directivity plots where, for each row from left to right, simulations were performed: 1) via the considered FW solver; 2) the unified model considering both electric and magnetic dipoles (Section III); and 3) the magnetic-dipoles-only model (Section II). Each row corresponds to a different frequency, as depicted at the left-hand-side column.

Fig. 7: Average error ∀φ ∈ [0◦, 360◦) between the FF directivity computed from the proposed unified model (Section III) and FW simulations, as well as between the latter and proposed model considering only magnetic dipoles (Section II).
Fig. 13: 3D normalized field intensity patterns: FW simulations (top row), the proposed NF model in Section III (bottom row).

Fig. 15: Radiation intensity (W/sr) of the optimized 2D PPW-fed metasurface antenna array, given by 0.5 r2/η ∥ ∥H(Ω; f) i ∥ ∥ 2 2 . The geometric parameters were optimized to provide coverage over the region φ ∈ [0◦, 90◦]× θ ∈ [0◦, 30◦] w.r.t. the antenna center. In each subfigure, the actuating current vector i was configured to maximize the radiation intensity at: i) φ = 30◦ and θ = 15◦, ii) φ = 60◦ and θ = 15◦, and iii) φ = 90◦ and θ = 15◦, to showcase the beamforming capability over the azimuth angle φ in the optimized region. Subfigures (a), (b), and (c) correspond to increasing number of metamaterials: N = 128, 256, and 512, respectively, while the number of feeds Nf = 25 and the power constraint Ptot = 10 W remained fixed.

Fig. 5: The layout used in the FW simulations (top view). N = 10 elliptic iris elements were randomly placed on the top plate, with each n-th element having major radius l1 = 3.6 mm and varying minor radius l2,n, as shown in the legend. The size of the PPW is the same as in the simulations in Section IV-C2 (L = 150 mm and h = 5.21 mm).

Fig. 3: Retrieved (dashed) and analytic (solid) polarizability components versus frequency for different slot lengths l2 in mm. The three subfigures correspond to αm xx, αm yy , and αe from left to right. For each l2 value, curves share the same color. The real part is shown without markers, while the imaginary part is indicated with circular markers.

Fig. 14: Real part of the voltage across the terminals of the thin-wire source (at feed 1 in Fig. 5). The black line corresponds to the value obtained from FW simulations, where a thin-wire current source was placed at the feed location. The lines with markers represent the predictions with the proposed model: squares account only for the self-interaction term in (63), triangles account for the coupling between the feeds via Gff , and circles account for both coupling mechanisms, i.e., Gff +GfK −1H̄f . The plate separation in the subfigures was set as: (a) h = 5.21 mm, and (b) h = 1 mm.

Fig. 12: Error in dB between the normalized field intensity pattern computed from NF FW simulations and the proposed NF and FF models in Section III. The error is plotted versus θ and averaged over φ ∈ [0◦, 360◦) with Nφ = 360, considering the distances r = 0.3, 0.6, and 0.9 m.

Fig. 11: Normalized field intensity patterns versus the elevation angle θ for the four azimuth planes: (a) φ = 0◦, (b) φ = 90◦, (c) φ = 180◦, and (d) φ = 270◦. The proposed NF model in Section III is compared against NF FW simulations, while the pattern retrieved from FF FW simulations is also plotted for reference.

Fig. 16: Radiation intensity patterns of the optimized 2D PPW-fed metasurface antenna for single-direction beamforming toward Ω = (60◦, 60◦), considering: (a) N = 128; (b) N = 256; and (c) N = 512 elements. In each case, the geometric parameters were optimized for the target direction, while the remaining system parameters were identical to those in Fig. 15.

Fig. 10: The absolute values of the scattered electric field in free space, retrieved via FW simulations without using the FF approximation, for distances r = 0.2, 0.3, 0.6, and 3.0 m (top row). The absolute difference between the FW simulated electric fields and those acquired using the proposed NF proposed model in Section III (bottom row).
